spec.title: Rocketman — Product Spec
spec.lede: An in-repo project hub that takes a product from idea to production: spec, plan, board, decisions, debugging, and docs live in your repo as structured data and render to one offline HTML file humans skim and agents act on. This is the source-of-truth PRD for the v0.1 launch.

sections.title: A project should be one file away

blocks.html: Coding agents can finally do real work — but they thrash without shared context, and humans don't read long markdown. Rocketman gives both a single living source of truth that travels with the code: it branches with it, reviews inside the PR, and merges with it.

The north star is legibility. A human should understand exactly where a project stands in ten seconds; an agent fleet should pick it up and act without re-deriving context. The hub is a generated, read-only view; the truth is structured data you edit and rebuild.

sections.title: Project context is scattered and stale

blocks.html: Teams hit three failure modes. Unread: specs rot in markdown nobody opens. Disconnected: the board lives in a SaaS tool, a context-switch away from the code. Amnesiac: every new agent session re-derives the same context, and decisions and debugging trails vanish into chat history.

Rocketman addresses all three by putting the whole project — [[spec-requirements|spec]], board, [[adr-05|decisions]], and [[bug-01|debugging trails]] — in the repo as structured data, rendered into a hub that's actually pleasant to read.

blocks.name: The Founder / PM
jobs: See exactly where the project stands in ten seconds, without reading a wall of text
Know what's blocked and what's next at a glance
Trust that the spec reflects what's actually being built

blocks.name: The Engineer
jobs: Find the decision and the debugging trail next to the code they explain
Move a task or record an ADR without leaving the repo
Never re-explain the project to a fresh agent session

blocks.name: The Agent Fleet
jobs: Load shared, structured context and act without re-deriving it
Pick ready tasks off the board as a work queue
Write results back safely under the single-writer rule

blocks.title: Skim the whole project fast
blocks.asIf: As a Founder/PM, I want a dashboard that summarizes project state so that I understand where things stand without reading every task.
ac: Given a project, when I open the hub, then a one-paragraph AI summary and a health score are the first thing I see.
Given blockers exist, when I view the dashboard, then they are surfaced with their reason.
Given any link in the summary, when I click it, then the relevant entity opens in a drawer.

blocks.title: Edit safely as an agent fleet
blocks.asIf: As an Agent Fleet, I want one writer to own all updates so that parallel work never corrupts the hub.
ac: Given sub-agents working in parallel, when they finish, then they return results to the parent rather than writing directly.
Given the parent writes results, when the build runs, then the hub regenerates deterministically.
Given any change, when it lands, then the activity feed records the actor with human-vs-agent provenance.

rows.text: Render the whole project to one self-contained, offline HTML file.
Keep structured data as the source of truth; the HTML is generated and read-only.
Eight views: dashboard, board, spec, roadmap, decisions, debug, docs, activity.
Show an AI summary line on every entity and bidirectional backlinks.
Record human-vs-agent provenance on every item and activity event.
Ship a Claude skill stack that walks idea → production.
Aggregate many repos into one read-only org rollup.

blocks.html: These are hard constraints, not preferences. The build enforces what it can.
Determinism: same data in, byte-identical hub out — so CI can fail a PR whose committed hub is stale ([[rm-03|the doctor]]). No fonts: system stack only, so it renders identically offline.

sections.title: Idea → production, as a skill stack

blocks.html: Rocketman ships a Claude skill stack that walks a product from a raw idea to a shipped, iterated product. Each stage reads from and writes back to the same PM/ data, so the hub is always current.

items.t: agents read the hub, not chat history

blocks.html: Not a hosted SaaS — it lives in your repo. Not a real-time multiplayer editor — edits go through data + Claude. Not a replacement for issue-tracker sync — that's an optional adapter ([[rm-04|the CLI]] + the mirror skill). The HTML is read-only; it is not an input surface.
Scope discipline: one version, final. Cross-repo rollup ([[rm-12|RM-12]]) and a live Fleet view ([[rm-11|RM-11]]) are explicit post-launch milestones, not hidden scope in v0.1.

Browser support: the theme uses oklch(); pre-2023 browsers fall back poorly ([[bug-03|BUG-3]]). Scale: very large projects may need pagination/archiving in the views. Merge conflicts: a single tasks.json can conflict — per-entity files are proposed in [[adr-07|ADR-7]].
Open question: should a local rocketman serve mode let the board write back to disk, or stay strictly read-only? Tracked in the decision backlog.

children.title: Add Rocketman to an existing repo
Write a PRD with the track
Run a build & verify on CI

doc-t1.title: Your first Rocketman project
doc-t1.html:
Install Rocketman, scaffold a hub, and open it.

npx @winsendotai/rocketman init
npx @winsendotai/rocketman build
open PM/index.html

init scaffolds PM/ with starter data and a CLAUDE.md. build merges your data into PM/index.html. open it — no server, no dependencies.

doc-h1.title: Add Rocketman to an existing repo
doc-h1.html:
Drop the hub into any repo without touching your stack.

cd your-repo
npx @winsendotai/rocketman init
git add PM/ .claude/

Commit PM/ alongside your code — it branches, reviews, and merges with the code, so project state never drifts from the branch it describes.

doc-h2.title: Write a PRD with the track
doc-h2.html:
Let the skill stack force the thinking, then generate the spec.

/rm-ideate   # forcing questions
/rm-prd      # writes PM/data/spec.json
/rm-plan     # lays out the board

doc-h3.title: Run a build & verify on CI
doc-h3.html:
Fail the PR if the committed hub is stale or any reference is broken.

node engine/build.mjs --check

The check rebuilds in memory and compares against the committed PM/index.html; a non-zero exit means someone forgot to rebuild.

doc-r1.title: Data schema
doc-r1.html:
Four files merge — flat — into one #pm-data island.

  • core.json — project, burn, people, epics, labels, columns
  • tasks.json — the board
  • spec.json — spec sections + docs
  • content.json — ADRs, debug logs, activity
{ "id": "rm-01", "col": "done", "owner": "opus",
  "summary": "...", "backlinks": ["adr-02"] }

doc-r2.title: CLI commands
doc-r2.html:
• rocketman init — scaffold PM/ into a repo
  • rocketman build — regenerate PM/index.html
  • rocketman new <task|adr|debug> — create an entity
  • rocketman doctor — validate data + references

doc-e1.title: Why HTML, not markdown
doc-e1.html:
Humans don't read long markdown, and raw HTML is terrible to diff and for agents to edit reliably.

Rocketman splits the two. Structured data is the source of truth — small, diffable, agent-writable. The HTML is a generated, read-only view. You edit data; the build renders the hub. Best of both worlds.

doc-e2.title: The single-writer rule
doc-e2.html:
When a parent agent allocates work to a fleet of sub-agents, concurrent writes corrupt shared state.

  • Sub-agents read the hub and return structured results
  • The parent is the sole writer back to PM/ data
  • Only the append-only activity log may be appended concurrently

Serializing writes through one parent keeps a fleet's output consistent and reviewable, with full provenance preserved.
